heading: Theft, destruction, concealment, mutilation, or alteration of records or papers; penalties
label: 20702 Theft, destruction, concealment, mutilation, or alteration of records or papers; penalties
text: Any person, whether or not an officer of election or custodian, who willfully steals, destroys, conceals, mutilates, or alters any record or paper required by to be retained and preserved shall be fined not more than $1,000 or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.section 20701 of this title
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 1974a of Title 42
